title: Owner Investment for Stock
event: Tran launches Tran Consulting Inc. by investing $25,000 cash in exchange for common stock. Prepare the entry.

entries.label: Cash in, stock out — no revenue anywhere

lines.label: cash received
lines.why: The business received cash — assets up.
lines.trap: Don't overthink the debit; the trap in this entry lives entirely on the credit side.

lines.label: contributed capital
lines.why: The owner got shares, so equity grows through contributed capital.
lines.trap: NOT revenue. Revenue comes from customers, never from owners funding the business.

memorize.body: Owner investments and revenues both increase equity — but only revenue touches the income statement.
Ask where the cash came FROM. Owner → Common Stock. Customer → revenue. Lender → Notes Payable.

questions.prompt: How much REVENUE does Tran Consulting recognize from this investment?

distractors.feedback: Cash arriving isn't automatically revenue. This cash came from the owner, not a customer — it's contributed capital.

build.scaffold: Ask where the cash came FROM. Owner → equity (Common Stock). Customer → revenue.
